questions.question: In paragraph 8, Teacher Zhang mentions that Ji-li “participates in educational reform”. What inference can be made about his motivation for bringing this up?

responses.content: She is scared of her dad because the cold eye stare that he gives her, she likes to be at school than staying at school.
SHE THINKS THERE COMING TO HER HOUSE AND SHE THNKS SHES GONNA GET EXPOSED.
She wants to be more educable so she can be smart

questions.question:
Read the following passage from paragraph 10:

“I closed my eyes. I saw Dad standing on a stage, his head bowed, his name written in large black letters, and then crossed out in red ink, on a sign hanging from his neck. I saw myself standing in the middle of the stage, facing thousands of people, condemning Dad for his crimes, raising my fist to lead the chant, ‘Down with Jiang Xi-reng.’ I saw Dad looking at me hopelessly, tears on his face.”

What does this suggest about Ji-li's relationship with her father?

responses.content: She always did good with addition and she participates in class.
She shares that she participate in class.
Cause he wants everyone to know that she is intelligent

questions.question: Which of the following inferences about the interrogation is best supported by the text?

responses.content: She cry because the dad was in tears and she feels bad for him.
She feels helpless and mad at her self.

questions.question: Which of the following best expresses a central idea of the excerpt?

responses.content: D. People in China had the same rights and protections during an interrogation as they would in a free democracy.

questions.question: Which sentence from the text most strongly supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: A. “These comrades from your father's work unit are just here to have a study session with you. It's nothing to worry about.”
